title: Fait d'Fait
content:

{{Quote|When the other racers left Tintel, they were heading straight for Azna; but I'm heading for Fait d'Fait. As of Tintel, I was in fourth place—right behind Seeg. If everything goes well on Fait d'Fait, I should make it to Azna right on Seeg's heels.|Siro Simito, report on the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade|<ref name="CRO" />}}
'''Fait d'Fait''' was a [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]] celestial body located in the [[Venaarian sector/Legends|Venaarian sector]]. Around [[3643 BBY]], criminal syndicates recruited an off-worlder to help them manipulate a [[Unidentified Fait d'Fait governor|governor]] of Fait d'Fait, who pursued anti–organized crime policies. Later, during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the [[Racer/Legends|racer]] [[Siro Simito]] visited Fait d'Fait on a detour during the [[Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade]] [[Starship/Legends|starship]] [[Star Rally|race]]. Simito, an undercover [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] agent, had been tasked with delivering a data module from the astronomical object [[Dahvil]] to a contact on Fait d'Fait—a handoff which Simito carried out smoothly despite being briefly accosted by a [[Unidentified Barabel mugger|Barabel mugger]] in Fait d'Fait's [[City/Legends|city]].

==Description==
{{Quote|Luckily, this delivery is to Fait d'Fait, a system not far off the Tintel to Azna hyperspace route.|Siro Simito, report on the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade|<ref name="CRO" />}}
Fait d'Fait was a terrestrial celestial body<ref name="CRO" /> located in the [[Fait d'Fait system/Legends|Fait d'Fait system]], a part of the [[Venaarian sector/Legends|Venaarian sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> in the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Trans-Hydian]] portion of<ref name="TEA" /> the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It lay at a distance of no more than a [[Standard day/Legends|day's]] [[Hyperspace/Legends|hyperspace]] travel with a Class 1 [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]] from<ref name="CRO" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]<ref name="EAOC" /> planet [[Azna]] and less than eight days of travel away from<ref name="CRO" /> the [[Iskin sector/Legends|Iskin sector]]<ref name="EAOC" /> world<ref name="CSWE Ord Dorlass">{{CSWECite|II|419|Ord Dorlass}}</ref> [[Ord Dorlass]].<ref name="CRO" />

==History==
===Dirt on the governor===
{{Gamemechanics|Star Wars: The Old Republic}}
Around [[3643 BBY]],<ref name="Date">Per the reasoning [[Template:TOR_dates|here]], Act I of ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'' takes place around [[3643 BBY]]. Since players of ''The Old Republic'' can assign their companions on the mission "A Slight Lean" independently of the main class storyline at any point during the [[Star Wars video games|game]], the mission must take place at some point around 3643 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]].</ref> the [[Unidentified Fait d'Fait governor|governor]] of Fait d'Fait ran a strong anti–organized crime platform. In response, a number of criminal syndicates looked for an off-worlder who could convince the [[Governor/Legends|governor]] to reconsider his policies. An individual affiliated with one of the sides in the ongoing [[Sith Wars/Legends|struggle]] between the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] and the [[Sith Empire (Post–Great Hyperspace War)|reconstituted Sith Empire]] tasked a companion to help the criminals. Eventually, the companion uncovered embarrassing information about the governor that would allow the grateful syndicates to manipulate the [[Politics/Legends|politician]] indefinitely.<ref name="A Slight Lean">{{TORcite|type=crewskill|Underworld Trading|A Slight Lean}}</ref>
{{Endgame}}

===Detour by a racer===
{{Quote|My contact meets me in the lobby of a cheap hotel. Everything goes smoothly, except for the fact that he was over an hour late. That doesn't sound like much—but it means that I have to recalculate my hyperspace jump. I know from experience that is going to add at least two hours.|Siro Simito, report on the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade|<ref name="CRO" />}}
[[File:Siro_Simito.jpg|thumb|right|180px|The racer Siro Simito made a secret delivery on Fait d'Fait on behalf of the Rebel Alliance.]]
At one point during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]<ref name="CRO" /> and between [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] and approximately [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]],<ref name="GCWDates">''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'' establishes that [[Siro Simito]] delivered the data module at one point between the beginning of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] and some [[Time/Legends|time]] shortly after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the onset of the Galactic Civil War and the immediate aftermath of the Battle of Yavin to [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] and [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]], respectively.</ref> the [[Racer/Legends|racer]] and undercover [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] operative [[Siro Simito]] was tasked by the Alliance to deliver a data module from<ref name="CRO" /> the Outer Rim Territories<ref name="EAOC" /> celestial body [[Dahvil]] to a contact on Fait d'Fait while participating in the popular<ref name="CRO" /> [[Standard year/Legends|annual]]<ref name="CSWE Fodro">{{CSWECite|I|284|Fodro}}</ref> [[Star Rally]] [[Star/Legends|interstellar]] racing event known as the [[Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade]]. Simito intentionally decreased the hyperdrive efficiency of his [[Axe (starship)|starship]] on the first legs of the race's route so that he would not later make a suspicious delay on the segment of the route where the racer planned to make a detour to Fait d'Fait from the [[Tintel–Azna hyperspace route|hyperlane]] connecting the celestial bodies [[Tintel]] and Azna.<ref name="CRO" />

On the fifteenth day of the race, Simito arrived on Fait d'Fait, docking his [[GAT-12 Skipray Blastboat|ship]] in a warehouse owned by [[Gevil Flan]]. The undercover Rebel agent then changed out his racer [[Clothing/Legends|clothes]] and into a plain [[Jumpsuit/Legends|coverall]] and, [[Weapon/Legends|arming]] himself with a [[Vibro-ax/Legends|vibro-ax]], went to the [[Hotel/Legends|hotel]] where he was supposed to meet his contact. Although the latter arrived more than an [[Standard hour/Legends|hour]] later, the handoff of the data module otherwise occurred smoothly.<ref name="CRO" />

===A short delay===
{{Quote|To make things worse, when I step out the door of the hotel, I'm accosted by a Barabel mugger.|Siro Simito, report on the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade|<ref name="CRO" />}}
Outside the hotel, however, Simito was accosted by a [[Unidentified Barabel mugger|Barabel mugger]], who demanded the racer turn over his [[Axe/Legends|axe]] and [[Credit chip/Legends|credits]]. Despite his background as a [[Realspace/Legends|space]] [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]], Simito was unwilling to engage in violence, so instead of simply attacking the [[Barabel/Legends|Barabel]] with his weapon, he began acting crazed and overly aggressive, yelling at the mugger and threatening to engage him in a [[Unarmed combat/Legends|fist fight]]. The Barabel, despite being armed with a [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]], became unnerved at the display and fled the scene, leaving Simito free to return to his [[Starship/Legends|starship]] and resume the race toward Azna.<ref name="CRO" />

The delay of Simito's contact on Fait d'Fait made the racer recalculate his hyperspace jump from the celestial body, ultimately causing him to arrive at Azna two hours later than expected. At some point by the [[Time/Legends|time]] the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] was fought, Rebel Alliance [[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] included Simito's report, which described the racer's visit to Fait d'Fait during the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade, in a [[Datafile/Legends|datafile]] detailing various Rebel operatives.<ref name="CRO" />

==Locations==
{{Quote|When I arrive here I dock inside a warehouse owned by Gevil Flan.|Siro Simito, report on the Dahvil-Fodro Hyperspace Promenade|<ref name="CRO" />}}
Fait d'Fait was the site of a [[City/Legends|city]] where both a warehouse owned by Gevil Flan and a cheap hotel were located.<ref name="CRO" />

==Behind the scenes==
Fait d'Fait was created by [[Chuck Truett]] and was introduced in ''[[Cracken's Rebel Operatives]]'', a [[1994]] [[West End Games]] sourcebook published for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="CRO" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Fait d'Fait [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the celestial body itself, in grid square O-7.<ref name="TEA" /> Fait d'Fait was mentioned in an Underworld Trading Crew Skill mission of the [[2011]] [[BioWare]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]], ''[[Star Wars: The Old Republic]]'', though the information regarding the mission's outcome was later removed from the game.<ref name="A Slight Lean" />
